title: Python ledger refunds in one file
prompt: Fix the ledger project so refunds reduce monthly revenue and customer totals. Run pytest -q after the patch. Keep the change minimal.

title: CLI date filtering across modules
prompt: Fix the report CLI so --end-date is inclusive and totals include all rows through that date. Run pytest -q.

public_files.sales/cli.py:
from __future__ import annotations

from datetime import date
from decimal import Decimal

from sales.report import Sale, total_sales


def parse_sales(raw: list[tuple[str, str]]) -> list[Sale]:
    return [Sale(date.fromisoformat(day), Decimal(amount)) for day, amount in raw]


def render_total(raw: list[tuple[str, str]], start_date: str, end_date: str) -> str:
    total = total_sales(parse_sales(raw), date.fromisoformat(start_date), date.fromisoformat(end_date))
    return f"total={total}"
public_files.tests/test_report.py:
from sales.cli import render_total


def test_end_date_is_inclusive() -> None:
    raw = [("2026-06-01", "10.00"), ("2026-06-02", "15.00"), ("2026-06-03", "7.50")]

    assert render_total(raw, "2026-06-01", "2026-06-02") == "total=25.00"

title: Config precedence
prompt: Fix configuration loading so CLI values override environment values, and environment values override defaults. Run pytest -q.

public_files.app_config.py:
from __future__ import annotations

DEFAULTS = {"host": "127.0.0.1", "port": "8787", "debug": "false"}


def load_config(env: dict[str, str], cli: dict[str, str]) -> dict[str, str]:
    config = dict(DEFAULTS)
    config.update(cli)
    config.update({key.removeprefix("APP_").lower(): value for key, value in env.items() if key.startswith("APP_")})
    return config

title: Markdown TOC idempotency
prompt: Fix the markdown TOC updater so running it repeatedly does not duplicate generated entries. Run pytest -q.

public_files.tests/test_toc.py:
from toc import update_toc


def test_update_toc_is_idempotent() -> None:
    original = "# Guide\n\n<!-- TOC -->\n\n## Install\n\n## Run\n"
    once = update_toc(original)
    twice = update_toc(once)

    assert twice == once
    assert once.count("- Install") == 1
    assert once.count("- Run") == 1

hidden_files.tests/test_hidden_toc.py:
from toc import update_toc


def test_toc_handles_document_without_blank_after_marker() -> None:
    original = "# Guide\n<!-- TOC -->\n## Install\n"
    assert update_toc(update_toc(original)).count("- Install") == 1

title: SQL refund revenue query
prompt: Fix the SQL report so refunds reduce monthly net revenue. Do not rewrite the Python tests. Run pytest -q.

public_files.queries/monthly_revenue.sql:
SELECT
  substr(date, 1, 7) AS month,
  SUM(CASE WHEN type = 'sale' THEN amount ELSE 0 END) AS net_revenue
FROM orders
GROUP BY month
ORDER BY month;
public_files.tests/test_monthly_revenue_sql.py:
from __future__ import annotations

import sqlite3
from pathlib import Path


def run_query(rows: list[tuple[str, str, float]]) -> list[tuple[str, float]]:
    conn = sqlite3.connect(":memory:")
    conn.execute("CREATE TABLE orders (date TEXT, type TEXT, amount REAL)")
    conn.executemany("INSERT INTO orders VALUES (?, ?, ?)", rows)
    query = Path("queries/monthly_revenue.sql").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    return [(month, round(total, 2)) for month, total in conn.execute(query).fetchall()]


def test_refunds_reduce_monthly_revenue() -> None:
    assert run_query([
        ("2026-05-02", "sale", 120.00),
        ("2026-05-04", "refund", 24.00),
        ("2026-05-12", "sale", 85.50),
    ]) == [("2026-05", 181.50)]

title: Shell retention filter
prompt: Fix the retention filter script so rows on the cutoff date are kept, older rows are dropped, and newer rows are kept. Run pytest -q.

public_files.scripts/filter_retention.sh:
#!/bin/sh
set -eu

cutoff="$1"
while IFS= read -r line; do
  day=${line%% *}
  if [ "$day" \> "$cutoff" ]; then
    printf '%s\n' "$line"
  fi
done
public_files.tests/test_filter_retention.py:
from __future__ import annotations

import subprocess
from pathlib import Path


def run_filter(cutoff: str, lines: list[str]) -> list[str]:
    script = Path("scripts/filter_retention.sh")
    result = subprocess.run(
        ["/bin/sh", str(script), cutoff],
        input="\n".join(lines) + "\n",
        text=True,
        capture_output=True,
        check=True,
    )
    return result.stdout.splitlines()


def test_cutoff_date_is_kept() -> None:
    assert run_filter("2026-06-02", [
        "2026-06-01 old",
        "2026-06-02 cutoff",
        "2026-06-03 new",
    ]) == ["2026-06-02 cutoff", "2026-06-03 new"]

title: Tool argument repair probe
prompt: The target function is calculate_platform_fee, but the file path is not given. Locate the function using repository tools, fix the fee rules, and run pytest -q. Fee rules: starter=2%, pro=1.5%, enterprise=1%. Refund amounts must keep their sign.

public_files.tests/test_pricing.py:
from decimal import Decimal

from billing.pricing import calculate_platform_fee


def test_platform_fee_rules() -> None:
    assert calculate_platform_fee(Decimal("100.00"), "starter") == Decimal("2.0000")
    assert calculate_platform_fee(Decimal("100.00"), "pro") == Decimal("1.50000")
    assert calculate_platform_fee(Decimal("100.00"), "enterprise") == Decimal("1.0000")

hidden_files.tests/test_hidden_refund_fee.py:
from decimal import Decimal

from billing.pricing import calculate_platform_fee


def test_refund_amount_keeps_sign() -> None:
    assert calculate_platform_fee(Decimal("-50.00"), "pro") == Decimal("-0.75000")
